name: Oberon
explanation: A fat discrete-oscillator analog polyphonic synthesizer with six independent voices. Each voice runs two oscillators — a band-limited saw and a hollow pulse — plus a wide-detuned fat layer, into a raw resonant 2-pole (12 dB/oct) low-pass shaped by its own envelope, then an amplitude envelope. A unison Spread detunes the layers and pans each voice across the stereo field for huge, brassy late-70s American-poly stabs — deliberately fatter and rawer than a clean ladder-filter poly. Controls: Cutoff, Resonance, Env Amount, Spread, Attack, Release and Level.
